title: pycreus flavescens subsp. tanaensis
intro: Pycreus flavescens subsp. tanaensis, also known as the Tana River Pycreus, is a type of sedge that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Pycreus flavescens subsp. tanaensis include the potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
